sections.title: Discipline
sections.content: The University shall, upon evidence of a student's failure to abide by its rules, regulations and code of conduct, take appropriate disciplinary measures against such an offender. Where a serious act of misconduct and/or indiscipline is involved, the matter shall be referred to the appropriate Disciplinary Committee for investigation and proper disciplinary action where necessary.

subsections.title: Decent Dressing
subsections.intro: The following are considered to constitute decent forms of dressing:
list_roman: i. Smart traditional attires which do not contravene the general dress code;
ii. Formal dressing for professional courses;
iii. Costumes worn during periods of performance;
iv. Religious or denominational dress worn during religious ceremonies only;
v. Aprons, overall, etc, prescribed by some Faculties /Departments and worn only when required;
vi. Sports and games clothing used when appropriate i.e., during sports/games meetings on the field.
list_alpha: a. Students should be well dressed at all times.
b. Dressing should be formal (suits, trousers and shirts, skirts and blouses, gowns or simple native attires).
c. Foot wears to lectures should be sandals, sneakers, canvas or covered shoes.
d. Face caps are not allowed in the classrooms.
e. Tattoos should not be exposed.
f. Dreadlocks should be neatly kept.
g. Beards should be kept short and neat.
h. Outrageous colours of hair attachments and dreads are prohibited (red, yellow, green, etc).
i. Sprayed or tinted hair for men is prohibited.
j. Sagging and ass-level trousers and skirts are prohibited.

subsections.title: Indecent Dressing
subsections.intro: The following types of dressing shall be considered indecent anywhere within the University and therefore prohibited:
list: i. DRESSES-Short and skimpy, e.g., body hugs, “show me your chest”, topless, spaghetti, sleeveless, one sleeve, see through, etc.
ii. SKIRTS-Tight –fitting, short (above the knees), long but tight with front, back or side slits that expose under-wear or sensitive parts of the body.
iii. TROUSERS-Hipsters, tight-fitting trousers exposing under –wear and the contours of the body. Tattered jeans or jeans with holes, short untidy trousers/jeans, as well as sagging etc.

iv. TOPS-Underclothing e.g., singlet worn to public functions, shirts/blouses with flying collars, without buttons or improperly buttoned; low-neckline blouses exposing sensitive parts of the body; short tops exposing the navel/belly.

v. APPEARANCE OF HEAD - Unkempt hair, head bands, wearing of ear-rings by male students, painting of the face to look like masquerades etc. perming, plaiting and weaving of hair by male students outside the hostels is highly prohibited.
vi. FOOT WEARS-Wearing of bathroom slippers outside the hostel e.g., to lectures and other public functions on campus (except on medical grounds).
vii. Wearing of shorts by both male and female students is prohibited outside the hostel.

subsections.title: Sanctions on Students' Indecent Dressing
subsections.note: Section numbering repeats 4.2.2 in source text.
list: I. Students shall not be allowed entrance into the University, lecture rooms, examination halls, health centre, library, offices and other University buildings.
ii. Any Student found guilty of indecent dressing shall be arraigned before the University Student Disciplinary Committee.
iii. Any student found guilty shall be suspended.

subsections.title: Sanction on Students guilty of Cybercrime
list: I. Any Student found guilty of cybercrime shall be arraigned before the University Student Disciplinary Committee.
ii. Any Student found guilty shall be expelled from the University.
